head.hi: Memorandum by the Secretary of State

div.p: During the call of the Italian AmbassadorNobile Giacomo de Martino. he asked me about the visit of M. Laval and whether that was going to undo all our policy of disarmament. I told the Ambassador, by no means, and I spoke of the good opinion I had formed of Laval during my visit to Europe and said that I thought his influence was for moderation in French policy. I called the Ambassador’s attention to my appointment of WilsonHugh R. Wilson, Minister in Switzerland. as a member of the Committee on the Grandi holiday proposalSee vol. i, pp. 440 ff. as evidence of my sincere desire to back them up.
